commands.review_summary:
Adds an opt-in `gateway.webchat.openResponsesProgress.mode="event"` setting, a WebChat-scoped `response.openclaw_progress` OpenResponses SSE event, docs, and focused config/Gateway tests.

PR surface: Source +66, Tests +119, Docs +37. Total +222 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level for the linked issue: current main emits structural SSE events early, while `response.output_text.delta` is only produced from assistant stream text or the post-run fallback. I did not run a live Gateway/WebChat timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer review for the new public config/SSE event contract and contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: feat(gateway): add opt-in WebChat OpenResponses progress event
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an opt-in WebChat-only OpenResponses progress SSE event for #85702.
- Keep `response.output_text.delta` reserved for real assistant text and the existing post-run fallback.
- Document the new config and event shape so clients can render early active state without treating progress as transcript text.

## Behavior

When `gateway.webchat.openResponsesProgress.mode` is set to `"event"` and a streaming `/v1/responses` request resolves to the WebChat channel, Gateway emits:

```json
{
  "type": "response.openclaw_progress",
  "response_id": "resp_...",
  "message": "OpenClaw is working on your request.",
  "created_at": 1780000000
}
```

The event is emitted after `response.in_progress` and before assistant text deltas. It is OpenClaw-specific metadata, not assistant transcript content. The default remains off, and non-WebChat OpenResponses clients do not receive the event.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm -s exec oxfmt docs/gateway/openresponses-http-api.md src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.gateway.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/gateway/open-responses.schema.ts src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts src/gateway/openresponses-http.ts src/gateway/server-http.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-config.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-state.ts src/gateway/server.impl.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm -s exec oxlint src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.gateway.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/gateway/open-responses.schema.ts src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts src/gateway/openresponses-http.ts src/gateway/server-http.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-config.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-state.ts src/gateway/server.impl.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm -s exec vitest run src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm -s exec vitest run src/config/config-misc.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm docs:list`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-bin:$PATH COREPACK_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-corepack corepack pnpm -s tsgo:core`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Streaming WebChat OpenResponses clients can receive an early non-transcript progress signal before assistant text is available, while default and non-WebChat streams keep the previous event sequence.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/webchat-openresponses-progress-event`, Node `v22.20.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the targeted OpenResponses Gateway test suite and config schema suite listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: `src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts` now proves default streams do not emit `response.openclaw_progress`, opt-in WebChat streams emit it after `response.in_progress` and before `response.output_text.delta`, and non-WebChat streams do not emit it. `src/config/config-misc.test.ts` proves `"event"` is accepted and unknown modes are rejected.

Observed result after fix: The new progress event is gated by `gateway.webchat.openResponsesProgress.mode="event"` and `messageChannel === "webchat"` without changing accumulated assistant text, final output, or fallback delta behavior.

What was not tested: A live deployed Gateway/WebChat browser timing run.

Fixes #85702
